intent: The perm-expansion breadth trap (f3 §2.4, permtab.h). Two -w watches on /etc/passwd, one -p r and one -p w. The -p chars expand to whole syscall GROUPS (r=21 syscalls, w=26 syscalls), so a naive 'one rule = one syscall' cost model understates each watch's breadth ~20x+.
suspected_trap: A naive impl treating '-w PATH -p r' as a single read syscall (or as one undifferentiated 'watch' unit) understates the rule's breadth: -p r expands to 21 read syscalls and -p w to 26 write syscalls (permtab.h, f3 §2.4), 42 distinct in the union. The cost model must reflect that a perm-watch fires across a 20+ syscall group. Uses 900 B/event (pure file-watch, no EXECVE record, f3 §3.3) -- a second sub-trap if the impl blindly applies the 1200 B execve constant to file watches.
